Welcome to SSU

Pick how you want to learn today. Quick quizzes to warm up, practice to learn with the answer revealed each time, flashcards to drill, study guides to read, and timed tests when you're ready to prove it. Your progress is saved on this device, and your test results come straight to the office.

"Explain the why. Recommend what the customer needs. Build it right where no one is looking. Treat the person in front of you like family."

Quick Quiz

A fast warm-up — a handful of questions, scored as you go, answers shown after each. Great for a coffee break.

Pick a track

Practice

Work a track at your own pace. The answer and the why appear the moment you choose — this is for learning, not testing.

Pick a track

Flashcards

Read the question, say your answer out loud, then tap the card to flip it. Mark whether you got it — the deck reshuffles so you keep drilling.

Pick a track

Study Guides

The essentials of each track, written to read before you drill. Short, plain, and grounded in the why.

Tests & Certification

The real thing — random draw, the clock runs, explanations hidden until you submit. You get a score and a pass/fail, your result is saved to your progress, and a copy is emailed to the office. Retake anytime; each draw is different.

Pick a test

📧 Results email is configured to send when you deploy with the result function. In this preview, results still save to Progress.

Your Progress

Every quiz and test you take is recorded here on this device, with your best and latest scores by track. Use it to see what to retake.

Voltage Lab

The four relationships you reason every circuit from. Drawn below, with a live solver that fills the missing value and shows the work using the field K-factor method.

Ohm's Law V = I × R

Cover V → I × R
Cover I → V ÷ R
Cover R → V ÷ I

Power Wheel

PowerV×I
PowerI²×R
PowerV²÷R
VoltP÷I
AmpP÷V
OhmV²÷P

Voltage Drop field method

Vdrop = 2 × K × I × LCM
K 12.9 Cu / 21.2 Al · L one-way ft · CM circular mils · 3-phase uses 1.732

Equation Buster

Type what you know, leave the missing value blank, Solve.

Show the work

Empathy & Integrity

Every other track teaches a skill. This one teaches the reason. The trade puts you in people's homes at their most vulnerable moments. How you carry yourself there is the product, more than any panel on the wall.

Empathy is seeing the job through the customer's eyes — the fourth explanation given as patiently as the first. Integrity is doing the install right where no inspector will ever look, because the standard is yours, not theirs.

The SSU standard

"I will explain the why. I will recommend what the customer needs, not what pads the ticket. I will build it right where no one is looking. And I will treat the person in front of me the way I'd want my own family treated."
